name: Mississippi John Hurt
description: American country blues singer and guitarist (1892-1966)
abstract: John Smith Hurt (March 8, 1893 – November 2, 1966), known as Mississippi John Hurt, was an American country blues singer, songwriter, and guitarist.

concerts.label: New York Folk Festival: The Contemporary Singer-Composers Part 1
New York Folk Festival: The Carl Sandburg American Songbag
New York Folk Festival: The Real Thing

works_created.t: Let the Mermaids Flirt with Me

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: I’ve got to do the second hour of the broadcast, and one of the songs I’m going to play by Doc Watson is [sings] Do you know John Hurt/Did you shake his hand.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Mancuso
about_quotes.evidence: you had these other, Samuel Charters and the rest of these guys who really elevated Mississippi — you know, John Hurt and all those older guys
about_quotes.doc_title: Chuck Mancuso interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 10, 2013
